1 Landing — Straight Into App
interest Confidence 0.40

"Okay, so this is TypingMind. 'The best frontend for LLMs' — interesting. It looks like a chat interface, kinda like ChatGPT but... different. I see GPT-5.4 in the model selector, that's cool. But wait, where's the pricing? Where do I learn what this actually does differently? There's a 'Buy a license' button up top but I want to understand the value prop first before I look at pricing."

⚠ No guided intro for first-time evaluators — raw chat UI is confusing for non-devs
2 Model Selector — Multi-Model
trust Confidence 0.50

"Oh wow, okay THIS is interesting. GPT-5.4, Gemini, Claude — all in one interface. That's actually a big deal for me. When I'm doing competitive analysis I use ChatGPT, but sometimes Claude gives better structured outputs for PRDs. Having them side by side... 'Custom models — Llama, Mistral, Perplexity — Need setup' — yeah that sounds like it needs API keys, my eng lead would have to help with that. But the main ones are right here. The '1M' and '400K' must be context windows? That's useful for feeding in long user research docs."

⚠ Context window numbers lack labels — non-technical users won't understand them
3 Pricing — Lifetime License
interest Confidence 0.60

"Okay, pricing! 'Get A Lifetime License — Buy once, use forever!' That's... actually really appealing. $39 Standard, $79 Extended, $99 Premium with 50% off. These are ONE-TIME payments? Not monthly subscriptions? That's wild. I'm paying $20/month for ChatGPT Plus, so in two months this would pay for itself. But wait — 'Price does not include API costs and additional cloud storage.' So I still have to pay OpenAI separately for the API? That's confusing. The Premium has 'Multi-model chats' and 'Projects & Folders' which I'd definitely need. But I need to understand the total cost picture."

⚠ API cost disclaimer buried — total ownership cost unclear for non-technical buyers
4 Plan Comparison Table
interest Confidence 0.70

"Oh nice, this is exactly what I needed — a proper docs page with a comparison table. Standard, Extended, Premium — all plans have chat with multiple models, manage chats, share chats, AI Agents, Prompts Library... that's a lot of overlap actually. I need to scroll down to see where they DIFFER. Also — I notice 'TypingMind vs ChatGPT Plus vs other ChatGPT UIs' in the sidebar. That's literally the comparison I'm trying to make in my head right now."

✓ Docs comparison table clarifies plan differences effectively
5 RAG & Knowledge Base
trust Confidence 0.75

"Okay NOW I can see the differences. Standard doesn't have Web Search, Text-to-Speech, Dall-E, or unlimited plugins — those are all red X marks. But the knowledge base thing — 'Connect with your data for RAG: Upload Files, Github, Google Drive, Notion, Web Scrape' — that's in ALL plans? That's actually huge for my workflow. I could connect our Notion workspace and have the AI reference our product docs directly. That's something ChatGPT doesn't do natively."

✓ Notion RAG integration available on all plans is a decisive PM workflow feature
6 Premium-Exclusive Features
joy Confidence 0.80

"Okay this is making my decision clearer. ALL plans support GPT, Claude, Gemini, DeepSeek, Grok — that's every major model. The real differentiator for Premium is 'Artifacts', 'Project Folders', and 'Canvas Editor'. Project Folders is a must-have for me — I need to organize by product initiative. And Artifacts sounds like Claude's artifact feature... Standard and Extended both miss those. So for my PM workflow, it's basically Premium or nothing. $99 lifetime for that? Compared to $20/month for ChatGPT Plus which only gives me one model... this is actually a no-brainer on price."

✓ Project Folders + Artifacts as Premium exclusives make $99 a clear PM value
7 TypingMind vs ChatGPT Plus
trust Confidence 0.85

"Okay THIS is the money page. TypingMind vs ChatGPT Plus side by side. Look at this — ChatGPT Plus has red X marks for Anthropic Claude, Mistral, Google Gemini, OpenRouter, Azure, Ollama, Perplexity... basically ChatGPT Plus only gives you GPT models. TypingMind gives you ALL of them. That's a massive advantage. When I'm doing competitive user research, I like to cross-check outputs between Claude and GPT to get different perspectives. With ChatGPT Plus I'd need separate subscriptions for each."

✓ Side-by-side ChatGPT Plus comparison confirms TypingMind's multi-model advantage
8 Settings — Local Data Privacy
trust Confidence 0.85

"Settings page. Okay so I see 'App Data & Storage' — 'All of your data is stored locally in your browser.' Wait — that's actually a big deal for privacy. The data doesn't leave my machine? That's way better than ChatGPT where everything goes to OpenAI's servers. 'Import From OpenAI' — nice, migration path. I see 'Model Context Protocol' under Advanced — that's the MCP thing I've been hearing about at product conferences. But I don't see a dedicated 'Security & Privacy' settings section here."

⚠ 'All data stored locally' is a major selling point buried deep in settings
9 API Keys Setup — BYOK Friction
interest Confidence 0.90

"Okay, API Keys page. 'By default, your API Key is stored locally on your browser and never sent anywhere else.' — that's the privacy story right there. Good. But looking at this... I need three separate API keys? OpenAI, Anthropic, AND Google? Each with their own 'Get API key here' links? This is where I'd deadass need to ask my engineering lead for help. I know what an API key IS, but going to three different platforms to generate keys, dealing with billing on each one... that's a LOT of setup friction for someone who just wants to chat with AI."

⚠ Managing 3 separate API keys across platforms requires engineering help for non-devs
10 Final Assessment
joy Confidence 0.90

"Alright, I've seen enough to make my evaluation. TypingMind is genuinely impressive from a feature standpoint — multi-model access, local data storage, Notion integration, prompt library, project folders. The $99 Premium lifetime deal is honestly a steal compared to $240/year for ChatGPT Plus. But the API key setup is going to be a speed bump. I'll need to ask Jake from engineering to help me get the keys set up. Once that's done though, I think this would be a significant upgrade to my PM workflow."

✓ Decision: buying Premium at $99 — will need eng help for API key setup
